Removed all mock.module() usage.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rt6z","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:27.331919665-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rwq0","title":"Task: E2E test - Touch gestures and bottom sheets","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:48:22.77212535-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:02:32.660657608-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:02:32.660657608-05:00","close_reason":"Added mobile bottom sheet drag-to-dismiss E2E test","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rwq0","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:58:16.487995466-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s5as","title":"Implement mobile-first responsive design","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-shm. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement mobile-first responsive design for all pages. ## Why This Matters From README: "We optimize for an elite UX across desktop and mobile." ## Responsive Breakpoints Using Tailwind's default breakpoints: - `sm`: 640px (large phones) - `md`: 768px (tablets) - `lg`: 1024px (laptops) - `xl`: 1280px (desktops) - `2xl`: 1536px (large desktops) ## Mobile-First Patterns ### 1. Navigation - Hamburger menu on mobile - Slide-out drawer for navigation - Bottom tab bar for primary actions - Collapsible header on scroll ### 2. Content Layout - Single column on mobile - Multi-column grid on desktop - Cards stack vertically on mobile - Touch-friendly spacing (min 44px targets) ### 3. Forms - Full-width inputs on mobile - Stack labels above inputs - Large touch targets for buttons - Keyboard-optimized input types ### 4. Tables - Horizontal scroll with sticky column - Or convert to card layout on mobile - Collapsible rows for detail ### 5. Modals - Full-screen on mobile - Slide-up sheet pattern - Easy dismiss with swipe - Fixed footer for actions ## Component Updates ### Mobile Header ```typescript // src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx export function MobileHeader() { return (
); } ``` ### Mobile Navigation ```typescript // src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx export function MobileNav({ isOpen, onClose }: Props) { return (
{/* Navigation items */}
); } ``` ### Responsive Grid ```typescript
{items.map(item => )}
``` ## Touch Interactions - Swipe gestures for navigation - Pull-to-refresh where appropriate - Long-press for context menus - Haptic feedback (where supported) ## Performance on Mobile - Lazy load images below fold - Reduce initial bundle for mobile - Optimize for slower connections - Respect data saver mode ## Testing Requirements - Test on actual devices (iOS Safari, Android Chrome) - Test in Playwright mobile viewport - Test touch interactions - Test with slow network throttling ## Files to Create - `src/components/layout/mobile-header.tsx` - `src/components/layout/mobile-nav.tsx` - `src/components/layout/mobile-tab-bar.tsx` - `src/components/ui/swipe-sheet.tsx` - `src/hooks/use-mobile.ts` ## Success Criteria - All pages usable on mobile - Touch targets are adequate size - Navigation is intuitive - Performance is acceptable on 3G - No horizontal overflow ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:35.477171694-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T12:59:34.333595982-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T12:59:34.333595982-05:00","close_reason":"Mobile-first responsive design is fully implemented: Nav with hamburger+drawer, BottomTabBar, SwipeablePromptCard, FloatingActionButton, PullToRefresh, useIsMobile/useHaptic hooks, responsive Tailwind breakpoints, touch targets, safe area handling."} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","title":"UI Color Consistency: Unify zinc/indigo to neutral palette","description":"# Epic: UI Color Consistency Overhaul ## Background & Problem Statement During the Stripe/Linear-inspired UI overhaul, we refactored core components (PromptCard, Hero, CategoryFilter, TagFilter, PromptGrid) to use a consistent `neutral-*` color palette. However, a comprehensive audit revealed that many other files still use the old `zinc-*` and `indigo-*` color schemes, creating visual inconsistency across the application. ### Current State (Inconsistent) - **Refactored components**: Use `neutral-*` (correct) - **page.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (14 instances) + `indigo-*` (6 instances) - **FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` + `indigo-*` - **PromptDetailModal.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (20+ instances) + `indigo-*` (5+ instances) - **SpotlightSearch.tsx**: Uses `indigo-600` for selected states - **SwipeablePromptCard.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` throughout - **BasketSidebar.tsx**: May have inconsistencies ### Why This Matters 1. **Visual cohesion**: Users perceive inconsistent colors as "unpolished" 2. **Dark mode**: `zinc` and `neutral` have slightly different hue values in OKLCH 3. **Maintainability**: Multiple color palettes = confusion for future changes 4. **Design system integrity**: We defined `neutral-*` as the standard ### Design Decision - **Primary text/bg**: `neutral-*` scale (consistent with Tailwind defaults) - **Accent colors**: Use CSS variables (`primary`, `muted`, etc.) when possible - **When `indigo-*` is needed**: Only for special emphasis (Pro badges, semantic search indicator) ### Success Criteria - [ ] Zero instances of `zinc-*` in component files (except intentional use) - [ ] `indigo-*` only used for specific Pro/premium features - [ ] All interactive states use `neutral-*` palette - [ ] Dark mode contrast maintained (WCAG AA) - [ ] Build passes with no visual regressions ### Files to Modify 1. apps/web/src/app/page.tsx 2. apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx 3. apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx 4. apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx 5. apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx 6. apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx (if needed) ### Estimated Scope - 6 files to modify - ~50-80 color class replacements - Low risk (visual only, no logic changes)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:19.74681817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:22:57.957413661-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:22:57.957413661-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 child tasks completed: page.tsx, FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx, PromptDetailModal.tsx, SpotlightSearch.tsx, SwipeablePromptCard.tsx, and BasketSidebar.tsx are all compliant with neutral color palette."} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-s6ex","title":"UI Color Consistency: Unify zinc/indigo to neutral palette","description":"# Epic: UI Color Consistency Overhaul ## Background & Problem Statement During the Stripe/Linear-inspired UI overhaul, we refactored core components (PromptCard, Hero, CategoryFilter, TagFilter, PromptGrid) to use a consistent `neutral-*` color palette. However, a comprehensive audit revealed that many other files still use the old `zinc-*` and `indigo-*` color schemes, creating visual inconsistency across the application. ### Current State (Inconsistent) - **Refactored components**: Use `neutral-*` (correct) - **page.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (14 instances) + `indigo-*` (6 instances) - **FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` + `indigo-*` - **PromptDetailModal.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` (20+ instances) + `indigo-*` (5+ instances) - **SpotlightSearch.tsx**: Uses `indigo-600` for selected states - **SwipeablePromptCard.tsx**: Uses `zinc-*` throughout - **BasketSidebar.tsx**: May have inconsistencies ### Why This Matters 1. **Visual cohesion**: Users perceive inconsistent colors as "unpolished" 2. **Dark mode**: `zinc` and `neutral` have slightly different hue values in OKLCH 3. **Maintainability**: Multiple color palettes = confusion for future changes 4. **Design system integrity**: We defined `neutral-*` as the standard ### Design Decision - **Primary text/bg**: `neutral-*` scale (consistent with Tailwind defaults) - **Accent colors**: Use CSS variables (`primary`, `muted`, etc.) when possible - **When `indigo-*` is needed**: Only for special emphasis (Pro badges, semantic search indicator) ### Success Criteria - [ ] Zero instances of `zinc-*` in component files (except intentional use) - [ ] `indigo-*` only used for specific Pro/premium features - [ ] All interactive states use `neutral-*` palette - [ ] Dark mode contrast maintained (WCAG AA) - [ ] Build passes with no visual regressions ### Files to Modify 1. apps/web/src/app/page.tsx 2. apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx 3. apps/web/src/components/PromptDetailModal.tsx 4. apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx 5. apps/web/src/components/mobile/SwipeablePromptCard.tsx 6. apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx (if needed) ### Estimated Scope - 6 files to modify - ~50-80 color class replacements - Low risk (visual only, no logic changes)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:34:19.74681817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:24:03.590434935-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:24:03.590434935-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 child tasks completed: page.tsx, FeaturedPromptsSection, PromptDetailModal, SpotlightSearch, SwipeablePromptCard, BasketSidebar. Zinc/indigo colors replaced with neutral palette."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-shi3","title":"Task: E2E test - Spotlight search and selection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:47:54.371332031-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:31.848158861-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:31.848158861-05:00","close_reason":"Already comprehensively implemented in e2e/web/spotlight.spec.ts - tests cover Cmd+K/Ctrl+K opening, Escape closing, typing to search, arrow key navigation, Enter to copy, 'No results' state, clicking results, and ARIA accessibility","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-shi3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:57:55.900113276-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-skwl","title":"Public site: add Pro CTA + Login in Nav","description":"## Goal Add a clear, tasteful path to Pro from the public site without undermining the free-first UX. Users should easily find: - **Go Pro** (primary CTA) - **Login** (secondary link for existing Pro users) ## Primary Placement - **Component:** `apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx` - **Entry point:** `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` renders `
` ## UX & Visual Requirements - **Free-first:** CTA should be visible but not dominant. - **Desktop:** - Secondary text link: **Login** - Primary button-like link: **Go Pro** (or **Upgrade** if copy fits better) - **Mobile:** - Add both links to the mobile sheet/menu. - **Go Pro** must be visually stronger than **Login**. - **Don’t break layout:** no wrapping or overflow at common widths. ## URL & Routing - Base URL: `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com` - CTA: `${PRO_URL}/` (Go Pro) - Login: `${PRO_URL}/login` ## Implementation Details 1) **Add a public env var** - `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` with default fallback to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`. - Update `apps/web/src/lib/env.ts` (or relevant env schema) to include it. 2) **Centralize URL usage** - In `Nav.tsx`, define: ```ts const PRO_URL = env.NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL ?? "https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com"; const proHref = `${PRO_URL}/`; const loginHref = `${PRO_URL}/login`; ``` 3) **Styling** - Use existing button/link styles in the nav to match the design system. - Prefer shadcn `Button` or existing Tailwind utility classes used in `Nav.tsx`. ## Analytics (Optional but Preferred) If analytics helper exists (e.g., `apps/web/src/lib/analytics.ts`), add events: - `pro_cta_clicked` (button) - `pro_login_clicked` (link) Attach context: `{ placement: "nav", device: "desktop|mobile" }` if available. ## Edge Cases - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` is missing, default to hardcoded `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`. - Ensure external link safety if using `target="_blank"`: include `rel="noopener noreferrer"`. ## Acceptance Criteria - Desktop and mobile nav both show **Go Pro** and **Login**. - Both links route to Pro site endpoints above. - Styles match the current nav and preserve free-first tone. - No layout regression on small screens. - (Optional) Analytics events fire on click. ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:12:19.562656095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:20:02.180861176-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:15:57.113388858-05:00","close_reason":"Added Pro CTA and Login links to Nav: desktop shows Login (secondary) and Go Pro button (primary with Crown icon) before basket; mobile menu shows same links in a separated section. Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL env var with fallback to pro.jeffreysprompts.com. All tests pass (488)."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-sqe","title":"Implement transcript processor (lib/transcript/processor.ts)","description":"Part 10.4 of PLAN. processTranscript(rawJsonl) parses JSONL, extracts messages/toolCalls/thinking, auto-detects sections via heuristics (ultrathink = deep thinking, clusters of Edit = implementation, etc), calculates stats (userMessages, assistantMessages, toolCalls, filesEdited, linesWritten, tokensUsed). Returns ProcessedTranscript for display.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:44:50.293889927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:37:50.793281326-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:37:50.793281326-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented transcript processor in apps/web/src/lib/transcript/processor.ts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-sqe","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:33.823124595-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
